mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with accuracy >55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient real economic activity that offsets higher borrowing costs, sustaining corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains during tightening periods yield negative 20-day forward returns in >58% of observations, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, creating immediate positive momentum for equity markets over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or predictive accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation acceleration above 0.4 percent for three consecutive months forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Inflation thresholds trigger positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validates synchronized demand across production and consumption channels, reinforcing earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate supply chain disruption or demand collapse, triggering rapid risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Sharp industrial output declines are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.
